# Ari Harrison

> Founder, NovoMCP. Building the open computational chemistry engine for drug discovery and materials science.

## Now

- Building: NovoMCP - the open computational chemistry engine. MCP + REST, 67 tools, self-hostable under Apache-2.0 or run as a per-org cloud deployment. Solo founder.
- Open source: NovoMD - local-first molecular descriptor library, MIT-licensed, ships as a Python library + CLI + Hugging Face MCP endpoint + Docker REST. https://github.com/ariharrisonlab/NovoMD
- Open to: product, AI, and 0-to-1 leadership conversations. Bay Area or remote. Particularly interested in production AI systems, MCP-shaped infrastructure, and applied AI at companies where correctness matters.
- Contact: arimgianni@gmail.com / LinkedIn (linkedin.com/in/ariharrisonlab).
- Backed by: NVIDIA Inception, AWS Activate, Microsoft Founders Hub.

## About

The interesting problem isn't getting a model to say something. It's building the system around the model so its output can be acted on - safely, correctly, repeatedly, with a record of what happened. That's the work.

NovoMCP is what I built. One computational chemistry engine, 67 in-silico tools across ADMET, docking, molecular dynamics, quantum chemistry, structure prediction, and compliance, that speaks both MCP (reachable from Claude Desktop, Cursor, Codex, Zed, and any MCP-compatible assistant) and REST (any pipeline that posts JSON). Two paths to run it: self-host it under Apache-2.0 on your own hardware, or run it as a per-org cloud deployment with the certified FAVES compliance API wrapped around it. Same engine either way.

The engine, never the platform (positioning call). Scale-to-zero by default (unit economics call). Human-in-the-loop via MCP elicitation, not autonomous-agent-on-someone-else's-molecules (trust call). Open-sourced as an engine, not as a hosted product (moat call - the moat is FAVES-cert + operated cloud, not the code).

Before NovoMCP: reinforcement learning agents for autonomous discovery in high-stakes manufacturing. The systems ran continuously, diagnosed their own failures, and improved without intervention. Different domain, same problem shape - the agent isn't the product; the system that makes the agent safe to deploy is the product. That's the throughline.

## The Stack

- The Engine: MCP + REST, 67 tools, ~14 always-available on a fresh clone
- License: Apache-2.0 (orchestration core BSL 1.1 -> Apache 2029-07-12)
- Compliance: FAVES V4 (hosted certified API) - 8 jurisdictions, 1,585 structural alerts
- Surfaces: MCP + REST + Workbench (v1.5.x) + Chrome ext + Word add-in + bundled dashboard
- Verticals: Drug discovery + materials science
- Ecosystem: NVIDIA Inception, AWS Activate, Microsoft Founders Hub

## Publications

Research on how domain structure shapes reasoning - and how reasoning systems adapt under different objective and constraint regimes.

- FAVES V4: A Production-Deployed Compliance and Liability Screening System for Drug Discovery at 122M-Compound Scale (2026.05, ChemRxiv) - Production compliance and liability screening aligned to HHS/ONC FAVES and NIST AI 600-1. Single-API call returns regulatory status (DEA / FDA / CWC / EPA / EU REACH), 1,585 structural alerts, BOILED-Egg permeability, and InChIKey prior-art lookup. Validated 362/362 compounds post-fix across five predetermined tests with documented governance bundle. DOI: 10.26434/chemrxiv.15003738/v3

- NovoExpert: State-of-the-Art ADMET Prediction on TDC Benchmarks (2026.04, ChemRxiv) - Five SOTA wins on Therapeutics Data Commons ADMET endpoints: CYP2D6 (0.778 AUPRC), CYP3A4 (0.916 AUPRC), CYP3A4 Substrate (0.648 AUPRC), Clearance Hepatocyte (0.602 Spearman), DILI (0.922 AUROC). CatBoost ensembles with MapLight and GIN fingerprints. DOI: 10.26434/chemrxiv.15000061/v2

## Projects

- NovoMCP - the open computational chemistry engine (https://github.com/NovoMCP/novomcp): One engine, two surfaces (MCP + REST). 67 tools across cheminformatics, ADMET, docking, MD, quantum chemistry, structure prediction, materials science, and an 11-stage autonomous discovery funnel. ~14 tools work with a fresh clone; the rest unlock as you wire optional compute services. Apache-2.0 top-level; orchestration core BSL 1.1 -> Apache 2029-07-12. Works with Claude Desktop, Cursor, Codex, Zed, ChatGPT, Gemini, and any MCP-compatible assistant, or hit it from your own code over HTTP. https://novomcp.com/engine

- FAVES certified compliance API: The moat around the OSS engine. Certified regulatory compliance for pharma / biotech pipelines - DEA schedules, EU REACH, PAINS filters, structural alerts, sponsor whitelists, PMDA / KFDA / TGA - plus the operational commitments a regulated submission needs (SLA, immutable audit-log retention, drift monitoring, IQ/OQ/PQ documentation, SAML SSO). Closed and paid; access on request. Not shipped with the OSS engine. https://novomcp.com/faves

- NovoWorkbench - native desktop for the engine (design-partner preview): The molecule canvas, 3D viewers, and discovery funnel as a native desktop for macOS and Windows. Local RDKit, offline alert screening (PAINS + druglikeness rule sets), bring-your-own-LLM chat, model-agnostic. Tauri + Python sidecar; Apple Developer ID notarized (team 8N9K9B7Y69). Currently in design-partner preview; ships as OSS in v1.5.x per the product roadmap. https://novomcp.com/novoworkbench

- Chrome extension (browser surface): Hover any SMILES on PubChem, ChEMBL, DrugBank, or preprints for instant ADMET, compliance, similarity search. Side panel for advanced compute. "Continue in AI" deep-links into MCP-connected assistants. Manifest V3, whitelisted hosts only. Chrome Web Store: nfnhfmfmaccidmepfhllcllnjopeonmf. Repo: https://github.com/NovoMCP/novomcp-chrome-sideload

- Word add-in (editor surface, private beta): Inline SMILES profiling and compliance tables in Word documents - manuscripts, protocols, grants. Office.js add-in, static-hosted manifest at addin.novomcp.com. Repo: https://github.com/NovoMCP/novomcp-word-sideload

- REST API (developer surface): Single endpoint shape - POST /v1/tools/{name} with Bearer key. Tool catalog is the OpenAPI 3.1 spec at api.novomcp.com/openapi.json. Same backend as MCP, zero code duplication. https://novomcp.com/api

- Bundled dashboard (control room): A minimal Next.js dashboard that ships bundled with the open engine at frontend-nextjs/ in the OSS repo. Runs on localhost when self-hosted; on the per-org subdomain in cloud deployments. Minimal today, growing with each release. https://github.com/NovoMCP/novomcp/tree/main/frontend-nextjs

- NovoMD (open source, MIT, https://github.com/ariharrisonlab/NovoMD): Local-first molecular descriptor library. SMILES in, 32+ descriptors out from a 3D conformer (geometry, energy, electrostatics, surface, volume), calculated on the user's own machine with no account and no API key. Four runtimes from one core: pip-installable Python library, CLI with batch/explain/report subcommands, a hosted Hugging Face MCP endpoint (quantnexusai-novomd.hf.space) that works with Claude / Cursor / Continue / any MCP client, and a Docker REST service. Deliberately scoped: no ADMET, no pKa, no binding affinity, no solubility predictions - that boundary is documented in the README and shipped as an agent skill telling AI assistants explicitly where the tool's authority ends. Determinism guarantee: same SMILES, identical descriptors, any machine.

- Product portfolio (https://ariharrisonlab.github.io/portfolio.html): Each surface as a product case - problem, decision, build, outcome. Plus cross-cutting decisions: engine-first language, scale-to-zero by default, Azure to AWS migration, private-endpoint infra, opening the engine at v1.0, and human-in-the-loop over autonomy.

## Writing

- Server-Side Tool Search for a 62-Tool MCP Platform (2026.04) - https://substack.com/home/post/p-195517589
- Why Most AI Pilots Fail: The Missing Infrastructure Layer (2026.02)

Writing available at: https://substack.com/@ariharrisonlab

## Leadership decisions

Selected product, infra, and positioning calls made shipping NovoMCP. Posted in full at https://ariharrisonlab.github.io/#leadership.

- Open the engine at v1.1.0: Apache-2.0 top-level, BSL 1.1 -> Apache 2029-07-12 for the orchestration core. The moat is FAVES certification + operated cloud + trained models, not the code that calls RDKit and orchestrates docking. Distribution flips from "sign up for our platform" to "clone the repo."
- Scale-to-zero by default: every compute service ships with min_replicas=0; only public-path surfaces stay warm. Unit economics stay honest day one.
- "Engine" never "platform": locked public language to "computational chemistry engine"; positioning rule rather than positioning deck.
- Killed the campaign / autonomous-run system. Replaced with human-in-the-loop via MCP elicitation at every funnel stage. The audit log is the quality gate.
- Self-hosted or cloud, no middle: killed the prior Free Trial / Core / Scale / Enterprise credit-tier ladder at the OSS launch. Two paths: self-hosted (free forever, Apache-2.0) or cloud deployment (contact for pricing). No self-serve middle tier.
- Private-endpoint-only infra (EKS API, Aurora, Redis, internal ALBs). Build on GH-hosted runners, deploy via OIDC -> SSM -> bastion. Pattern applied uniformly across every service.
